ripenessTips: Stem should be slightly dry; flesh yields gently when pressed; strong sweet aroma. The signature star pattern on the bottom of the husk should be clearly visible.
colorIndicators: Bright yellow to golden flesh; husk remains greenish-brown even when ripe.
tasteProfile: Rich, creamy, bittersweet with deep caramel notes

ripenessTips: Check the thorn at the base; should be dark and firm. The husk should give a hollow sound when tapped.
colorIndicators: Orange to deep amber flesh; outer husk is brownish-green with dark-tipped thorns.
tasteProfile: Intensely sweet with subtle alcoholic undertone and smoky finish

ripenessTips: Husk should feel hollow when shaken; flesh is deeply coloured and aromatic. Look for a reddish tinge at the stem.
colorIndicators: Orange-red to deep coral flesh; husk is yellowish-brown with reddish tones.
tasteProfile: Sweet and mildly bitter with floral honey notes

ripenessTips: Strong fermented wine-like smell; husk is slightly browned at stem. Best eaten slightly chilled.
colorIndicators: Pale yellow to light cream flesh; husk turns brown when ripe.
texture: Watery, soft, almost pudding-like when fully ripe
tasteProfile: Bitter-sweet with a distinctive fermented, alcoholic aftertaste

ripenessTips: Thorns should be close together; sweet earthy aroma; flesh is firm yet yielding. Avoid if husk is cracked.
colorIndicators: Pale yellow to light gold flesh; husk is green to olive-brown.
tasteProfile: Balanced sweetness with mild bitterness and nutty undertones

ripenessTips: Stem is thick and woody; light pleasant aroma; flesh should spring back when pressed. The thorns are large and pyramidal.
colorIndicators: Pale yellow to bright yellow flesh; husk is greenish-yellow even when ripe.
tasteProfile: Mildly sweet, milky, with vanilla undertones

ripenessTips: Strong pungent aroma; flesh is soft and deeply coloured; husk is brownish-green. One of the easiest to identify by smell alone.
colorIndicators: Deep yellow to orange flesh; husk darkens to brown when ripe.
tasteProfile: Rich sweetness with nutty and slightly bitter aftertaste

ripenessTips: Small round shape with blunt thorns; gentle sweet smell; flesh dents slightly when pressed. Ripens faster than most varieties.
colorIndicators: Light yellow to ivory flesh; husk stays relatively green.
tasteProfile: Sweet and floral with a clean finish

ripenessTips: Star-shaped pattern at the base is prominent; strong aroma; flesh is intensely yellow. The husk is slightly oval.
colorIndicators: Deep golden yellow to orange flesh; husk is yellow-brown.
tasteProfile: Very sweet with turmeric-like earthiness and caramel notes

ripenessTips: Husk is pale green to brown; mild aroma; flesh yields easily to touch. Best consumed within a day of falling.
colorIndicators: Pale cream to light yellow flesh; husk is light green to tan.
tasteProfile: Mild sweetness with a savoury, almost cheesy note

ripenessTips: Medium-sized oval shape; reddish tinge on husk; pleasant fruity aroma. Flesh separates cleanly from the seed.
colorIndicators: Orange to reddish-yellow flesh; husk has reddish patches.
tasteProfile: Sweet and slightly sour with fruity undertones

ripenessTips: Base thorn is dark brown to black; alcoholic aroma when ripe; flesh is deeply coloured. Handle carefully due to sharp thorns.
colorIndicators: Deep orange to burnt amber flesh; husk is dark brown with black-tipped thorns.
tasteProfile: Complex sweetness with wine-like depth and smoky aroma

ripenessTips: Greenish husk even when ripe; mild sweet scent; flesh is tender. The thorns are relatively short and blunt.
colorIndicators: Pale yellow with greenish tint flesh; husk stays green.
tasteProfile: Sweet with subtle grassy and herbal notes

ripenessTips: Flat base and round shape; strong nutty aroma; flesh is bright orange. The husk is relatively smooth compared to others.
colorIndicators: Deep orange to rust flesh; husk is olive-brown.
tasteProfile: Sweet, nutty, with hints of peanut butter

ripenessTips: Thorns are thick and stubby; rich buttery aroma; flesh is glossy yellow. The bottom is slightly concave.
colorIndicators: Bright golden yellow flesh; husk is yellow-brown.
tasteProfile: Creamy sweetness with vanilla and almond undertones

ripenessTips: Long oval shape; delicate floral smell; flesh is pale and firm when ripe. The thorns are fine and pointed.
colorIndicators: Pale cream to light yellow flesh; husk is greenish-brown.
tasteProfile: Floral sweetness with a light, tea-like fragrance

ripenessTips: Gourd-like shape; bitter almond smell; flesh is pale and firm. Best eaten when slightly under-ripe for texture.
colorIndicators: Pale yellow to ivory flesh; husk is brownish-green.
tasteProfile: Bitter-sweet with complex layers and a dry finish

ripenessTips: Small size with dense thorns; syrupy sweet aroma; flesh is deeply red-orange. Often sold in pairs.
colorIndicators: Deep orange-red flesh; husk is yellow with reddish spots.
texture: Very soft, almost liquid when fully ripe
tasteProfile: Intensely sweet with a hint of rose and syrup

ripenessTips: Greenish husk persists when ripe; light fresh aroma; flesh is springy to touch. Named for its bamboo-green appearance.
colorIndicators: Pale greenish-yellow flesh; husk is distinctly green even at peak ripeness.
tasteProfile: Mild sweetness with fresh, vegetal notes

ripenessTips: Uniform shape; strong sweet aroma; flesh yields gently with slight bounce. Developed for consistent quality year-round.
colorIndicators: Yellow to golden flesh; husk is medium brown.
tasteProfile: Balanced sweet-bitter with buttery richness
